questions.text: The octet rule states that atoms combine to achieve _____ electrons in their valence shell.
questions.explanation: To achieve stable noble gas electronic configuration.

questions.text: Which molecule has a triple bond?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen atoms share three pairs of electrons (N≡N).

questions.text: According to VSEPR theory, the shape of a CH₄ molecule is:
questions.explanation: Four bond pairs and zero lone pairs lead to a tetrahedral arrangement.

questions.text: The hybridization of Carbon in Ethene (C₂H₄) is:
questions.explanation: Carbon forms one double bond and two single bonds.

questions.text: Which of the following has a net dipole moment of zero?
questions.explanation: CO₂ is linear; bond dipoles cancel each other out.

questions.text: The formal charge on the central Oxygen atom in Ozone (O₃) is:
questions.explanation: Calculated as: Valence electrons - Lone pair electrons - ½(Bond pair electrons).

questions.text: In Metallic bonding, the electrons are:
questions.explanation: The 'sea of electrons' model explains conductivity and malleability.

questions.text: Which theory explains the paramagnetic nature of Oxygen (O₂)?
questions.explanation: MOT shows two unpaired electrons in antibonding π* orbitals.

questions.text: The bond order of He₂ molecule is:
questions.explanation: Bond order = ½(2 - 2) = 0. Therefore, He₂ does not exist.

questions.text: What type of hybridization is present in PCl₅?
questions.explanation: Phosphorus expands its octet using d-orbitals to form 5 sigma bonds.

questions.text: Sigma (σ) bonds are formed by _____ overlap.
questions.explanation: Occurs along the internuclear axis.

questions.text: Pi (π) bonds are formed by _____ overlap.
questions.explanation: Overlapping of p-orbitals perpendicular to the axis.

questions.text: Shape of NH₃ molecule is:
questions.explanation: Three bond pairs and one lone pair.

questions.text: Bond angle in H₂O is approximately:
questions.explanation: Lone pair-Lone pair repulsion reduces the angle from 109.5°.

questions.text: Which is the strongest bond?
questions.explanation: Generally, ionic bonds have higher lattice energy than covalent bond energy.

questions.text: Lattice enthalpy is the energy required to:
options: Form one mole of ionic solid
Separate one mole of ionic solid into gaseous ions
questions.explanation: It measures the strength of ionic interaction.

questions.text: Which molecule has an expanded octet?
questions.explanation: Sulfur has 12 electrons in its valence shell in SF₆.

questions.text: Fajan's rules describe:
questions.explanation: Polarization of anions by cations leads to covalent character.

questions.text: Intramolecular Hydrogen bonding is found in:
questions.explanation: Bonding occurs within the same molecule.

questions.text: Molecular orbital configuration of N₂ has _____ electrons in bonding orbitals.
questions.explanation: σ1s² σ*1s² σ2s² σ*2s² (π2px²=π2py²) σ2pz².

questions.text: Bond length is inversely proportional to:
questions.explanation: Higher bond order (triple > double > single) means shorter bond length.

questions.text: Hybridization of Be in BeCl₂ is:
questions.explanation: Linear shape implies sp hybridization.

questions.text: The number of non-bonding electron pairs in H₂O is:
questions.explanation: Oxygen has 6 valence electrons; 2 used for bonding, 4 left as 2 lone pairs.

questions.text: Which molecule is linear?
questions.explanation: Ethyne (acetylene) has sp hybridization.

questions.text: Coordination bond is also known as:
questions.explanation: Both electrons in the shared pair come from one atom.

questions.text: Energy released when a bond is formed is:
questions.explanation: Bond formation is exothermic.

questions.text: Resonance occurs in:
questions.explanation: Carbonate ion can be represented by multiple equivalent Lewis structures.

questions.text: Which shows highest boiling point due to hydrogen bonding?
questions.explanation: Fluorine is most electronegative, forming strongest H-bonds.

questions.text: Bond angle in sp² hybridization is:
questions.explanation: Trigonal planar geometry.

questions.text: Molecular Orbital Theory was developed by:
questions.explanation: Basic history of chemical bonding.

questions.text: Paramagnetism is due to:
questions.explanation: Unpaired electrons have a net magnetic moment.

questions.text: Which bond is polar?
questions.explanation: Difference in electronegativity creates a dipole.

questions.text: Bond order of O₂⁺ compared to O₂ is:
questions.explanation: Removing an electron from antibonding orbital increases bond order (2.5 vs 2).

questions.text: Shape of PCl₃ is:
questions.explanation: Similar to NH₃; one lone pair on P.

questions.text: Lone pair - lone pair repulsion is _____ than bond pair - bond pair.
questions.explanation: Lone pairs occupy more space.

questions.text: Valence Bond Theory (VBT) focuses on:
questions.explanation: Proposed by Heitler and London.

questions.text: Antibonding orbitals have _____ energy than bonding orbitals.
questions.explanation: Due to nodal planes and electron-electron repulsion.

questions.text: Which is not a type of Van der Waals force?
questions.explanation: Ion-Dipole is a stronger electrostatic interaction, usually categorized separately.

questions.text: Electronic configuration of O₂ molecule ends in:
questions.explanation: Two unpaired electrons in π* orbitals.

questions.text: Dipole moment is a _____ quantity.
questions.explanation: It has magnitude and direction (from + to -).
